name: Red-Cooked Chicken
recipeIngredient: Water to cover 3/4 of the way
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and pat dry the chicken.
2. Combine the soy sauce, dark soy sauce, dry sherry, garlic, scallions, water (to cover 3/4 of the container), cornstarch, and sesame oil in a bowl.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil.
4. Add the chicken and enough water to cover three-quarters of the container. Lower the heat and let simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. After 45 minutes, turn the chicken breast side down and continue cooking for another half hour until done.
6. Remove the chicken, disjoin it if necessary, and arrange on a platter.
7. Strain and defat the liquid, reserving all but about 2 cups for another use.
8. Bring the reserved liquid to a boil and reduce by half.
9. Add the cornstarch mixed with water. Cook until thickened, adjusting seasoning with salt as needed.
10. Pour the sauce over the chicken and flick a few drops of sesame oil on top.
11. Serve hot or cold.
